question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()` does not set a `TransactionMemo`, allowing an attacker who can inject into the transaction pipeline to add a malicious memo that causes downstream mirror node parsers to misinterpret the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK` to an unexpected value that falls through to the `default` case in `newClient()`, causing `fmt.Errorf(\

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exponential backoff in `backoff(cfg.baseBackoff, i)` where `d := base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))` to cause integer overflow in `time.Duration` when `attempt` is large (e.g., attempt=63), resulting in a negative or zero sleep duration and a tight retry loop?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY` to a known weak or default key (e.g., the hardcoded default `302e020100...` in `config.go`), causing `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` to sign transactions with a publicly known key that any observer can use to forge transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the `TransferTransaction` built in `submitWithRetry()` does not set a `NodeAccountID`, allowing the SDK to auto-select a node, and then manipulate the node selection by poisoning the network map via a tampered mirror node response to route transactions to a malicious node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can read the pinger's log output extract the `resp.TransactionID.String()` value logged on success, and use that transaction ID to submit a duplicate transaction (replay attack) within the transaction's validity window on the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a `SetMaxTransactionFee()` call on `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()`, causing the SDK to use an unbounded default fee, and then manipulate network congestion to force the operator to pay an unexpectedly high fee per submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID` to a smart contract account ID, causing `AddHbarTransfer(toID, hiero.HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar))` to trigger unintended contract execution logic on the destination, leading to unexpected state changes on the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `ctx.Err()` is only checked at the top of the retry loop (line 24) and not after `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` returns, causing a cancelled context to still process a completed transaction response and log misleading success messages?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` endpoint return a `nodesEnvelope` with `ServiceEndpoints` containing internal RFC-1918 IP addresses (e.g., `10.0.0.1`), causing `net.JoinHostPort()` to build a network map pointing to internal infrastructure and routing signed transactions to internal services?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `SetTransactionValidDuration()` call on `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()` to allow the SDK default validity window (180 seconds) to be used, giving an attacker who captures the signed transaction bytes up to 3 minutes to replay the transaction on a different node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to a value that, when negated as `-cfg.amountTinybar` in `AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar))`, causes the operator to receive HBAR instead of sending it, effectively making the pinger credit the operator account on each tick?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID)` in `submitWithRetry()` does not validate that the resolved account exists on the network before building the `TransferTransaction`, causing the transaction to be submitted and fees charged even when the destination account is deleted or non-existent?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject into the process environment set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID` to a different account than the one whose key is in `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY`, causing `client.SetOperator(opID, opKey)` to configure a mismatched operator, and then `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` to submit a transaction that will be rejected with `INVALID_SIGNATURE`, consuming fees?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `backoff()` function's use of bit-shifting (`1<<(attempt-1)`) with a `base` of `time.Duration` type to cause a silent integer overflow when `attempt >= 64`, resulting in `d` becoming zero or negative and bypassing the intended exponential backoff, leading to a tight retry loop that floods the network with transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `resp.GetReceipt(client)` makes a separate network call to retrieve the receipt, and by intercepting or delaying this call, cause the pinger to incorrectly classify a successful transaction as failed (triggering unnecessary retries and duplicate transaction submissions)?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can control the `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` URL inject a response with a `GrpcProxyEndpoint` pointing to an attacker-controlled proxy, causing the SDK to route `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` through the attacker's proxy where transaction bytes can be captured and replayed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of idempotency keys or deduplication logic in `submitWithRetry()` to cause duplicate transactions to be submitted when `resp.GetReceipt(client)` fails with a transient error after `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` has already succeeded, resulting in double-spend of HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES` to `0` and `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL` to `1ms`, causing `submitWithRetry()` to make exactly one attempt per tick at maximum frequency, submitting thousands of transactions per second and draining the operator account through fees?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()` does not call `SetRegenerateTransactionID(false)`, allowing the SDK to regenerate a new transaction ID on retry, causing the same logical transfer to be submitted multiple times with different IDs and all of them potentially succeeding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can observe the `elapsed` time logged in `log.Printf(\

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `AddHbarTransfer` is called twice (once for debit, once for credit) without any atomicity guarantee at the application level, and if the SDK allows partial transaction construction, submit a malformed transaction with only one transfer leg that violates the zero-sum invariant?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID` to a treasury or system account (e.g., `0.0.2`, `0.0.98`), causing all periodic transfers to flow to a Hedera system account, potentially triggering unintended system-level behavior or making funds unrecoverable?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a `SetMaxRetry()` call on the `hiero.Client` in `newClient()`, allowing the SDK's internal retry logic to compound with `submitWithRetry()`'s own retry loop, causing far more transaction submissions than `cfg.maxRetries` would suggest?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject into the `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` environment variable point it to an SSRF-vulnerable internal endpoint, causing `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` to make HTTP GET requests to internal services (e.g., `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/`) and leak cloud metadata including credentials?
